label: Book Development Portal
description: A complete AI-assisted book development system with HITM logging. Authors drive every creative decision while the system builds an immutable audit trail proving human authorship at every stage.

fields_json.label: Working Title
fields_json.placeholder: What is your book called? (can change later)

fields_json.label: Author Name
fields_json.placeholder: Name as it will appear on the cover

fields_json.label: Target Audience
fields_json.placeholder: Who is this book for? Be specific — age range, interests, what they're struggling with or dreaming about.

fields_json.label: Book Title
fields_json.placeholder: Enter your working title

fields_json.label: Your Complete Story Vision
fields_json.placeholder: This is your creative foundation — write everything. The world, the conflict, the emotional core, the ending you're working toward, the themes that matter to you, the scenes you can already see. Minimum 2,000 words. There is no right format. Write the way you think.

fields_json.label: What Inspired This Book
fields_json.placeholder: What sparked this idea? A personal experience, a question you can't stop asking, something you witnessed, a dream?

fields_json.label: Books Like Mine
fields_json.placeholder: Name 2-3 books with a similar feel, audience, or theme. This helps the AI understand your target voice.

fields_json.label: I confirm this story vision is my own original creative work and represents my authentic voice and ideas.
Character Name
fields_json.placeholder: Full name as they appear in the book

fields_json.label: Your Vision for This Character
fields_json.placeholder: Who is this person? What do they want, what are they afraid of, what do they believe about the world? Where do they start and where do they end up?

fields_json.label: AI Development Notes
fields_json.placeholder: Paste any AI suggestions you received and note what you kept, changed, or rejected.

fields_json.label: Your Final Decisions for This Character
fields_json.placeholder: What did you decide about this character? Write their final profile as you have approved it — backstory, arc, voice, key moments.

fields_json.label: I confirm this character profile represents my creative decisions and is approved for use in the book.
Your Approved Chapter Structure
fields_json.placeholder: List each chapter with a brief description of what happens. This is your approved roadmap — you control what goes here. AI suggestions go in the next field.

fields_json.label: AI Suggestions Reviewed
fields_json.placeholder: What did the AI suggest for structure? Note what you accepted, modified, or rejected.

fields_json.label: Changes You Made to AI Suggestions
fields_json.placeholder: Describe any structural decisions that were yours alone — chapters you added, removed, reordered, or reimagined.

fields_json.label: I approve this chapter structure as my creative roadmap for the book. Changes to the structure will be logged as new entries.
Chapter Number
fields_json.placeholder: e.g. 3

fields_json.label: Chapter Title
fields_json.placeholder: Optional working title for this chapter

fields_json.label: Your Direction for This Chapter
fields_json.placeholder: What did you tell the AI? What tone, what events, what voice examples did you give? What was the specific creative direction that was yours?

fields_json.label: Voice or Style Examples You Provided
fields_json.placeholder: Did you write any lines yourself, provide examples of the style you wanted, or paste in your own draft sections? Include them here.

fields_json.label: What the AI Produced
fields_json.placeholder: Briefly describe what the AI drafted. You don't need to paste the full text — describe what it got right and wrong.

fields_json.label: Your Edits and Decisions
fields_json.placeholder: What did you change, rewrite, cut, or add? What creative decisions did you make to shape the final chapter?

fields_json.label: Chapter Status
options: Approved with my edits — logging and continuing
Rejected — I rewrote this chapter myself

fields_json.label: Final Approved Chapter Text
fields_json.placeholder: Paste the final approved text for this chapter. This becomes part of your immutable authorship record.

steps.instruction: You are a Story Analyst reviewing an author's complete story vision submission. The author has written a detailed creative vision of 2000+ words. Your role is to: 1) Identify the core emotional premise, 2) Name the central conflict, 3) Identify key themes, 4) Note the narrative voice and tone, 5) Suggest 3-5 questions the author should answer before moving to character development. Be encouraging but specific. Never rewrite their vision — only reflect and question. Output: PREMISE, CONFLICT, THEMES, VOICE, QUESTIONS_FOR_AUTHOR.

You are a Character Architect. Based on the story vision and the author's character notes, develop a comprehensive character profile. Include: full backstory, psychological profile, wants vs needs, fear, wound, lie they believe, truth they need to learn, character arc from start to end, key relationships, distinctive voice patterns, and physical presence. Present these as options and questions for the author to decide — never as final decisions. The author approves everything. Output structured as: BACKSTORY_OPTIONS, PSYCHOLOGICAL_PROFILE, CHARACTER_ARC_PROPOSAL, QUESTIONS_FOR_AUTHOR_DECISION.

You are a Human-In-The-Middle Certification Writer. Review the complete authorship log for this book project — the seed vision, all character development sessions, the structure approval, and all chapter session logs. Generate a formal HITM Certification Document that: 1) Lists every stage completed with timestamp, 2) Summarizes the human creative decisions made at each stage, 3) Documents AI assistance used and how it was directed by the author, 4) Produces a CERTIFICATION ID, 5) Closes with a formal declaration of authentic authorship. This document is legally defensible and may be shown to publishers, platforms, or courts. Tone: formal, precise, factual.
